**Pargat Singh** (born 5 March 1965) is a hockey player turned politician in [India](/wiki/India "India") and belongs to the [Indian National Congress](/wiki/Indian_National_Congress "Indian National Congress") in [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab "Punjab"). He is a former Indian [hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") player and his playing position was full back. He captained [Indian men's hockey team](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") at [1992 Barcelona Olympics](/wiki/1992_Summer_Olympics "1992 Summer Olympics") and [1996 Atlanta Olympics](/wiki/1996_Summer_Olympics "1996 Summer Olympics"). He has worked as an SP with [Punjab police](/wiki/Punjab_Police_(India) "Punjab Police (India)") before taking a plunge into politics.

## Champions Trophy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pargat_Singh&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Champions Trophy")]

### 1985 (Perth)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pargat_Singh&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 1985 (Perth)")]

[India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") vs [Germany](/wiki/Germany_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Germany men's national field hockey team"): At one point of time score board was ticking 1–5. Pargat Singh came forward and scored *4 goals in the last 6 minutes*. The result was a draw but it made Pargat Singh known.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

### 1986 (Karachi)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pargat_Singh&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1986 (Karachi)")]

He played against [Holland](/wiki/Netherlands_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "Netherlands men's national field hockey team") to give India a 3–2 victory.

## Olympics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pargat_Singh&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Olympics")]

He captained the [Indian men's hockey team](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_field_hockey_team "India men's national field hockey team") at the [1992 Barcelona Olympics](/wiki/1992_Summer_Olympics "1992 Summer Olympics") and the [1996 Atlanta Olympics](/wiki/1996_Summer_Olympics "1996 Summer Olympics").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Political career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pargat_Singh&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Political career")]

In 2003, he was vice-president of the Surjit Singh Memorial Hockey Tournament Society of [Jalandhar](/wiki/Jalandhar "Jalandhar").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Shiromani Akali Dal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pargat_Singh&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Shiromani Akali Dal")]

Singh was nominated[*[when?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] for [Jalandhar Cantonment Assembly constituency](/wiki/Jalandhar_Cantonment_Assembly_constituency "Jalandhar Cantonment Assembly constituency") seat as [SAD](/wiki/Shiromani_Akali_Dal "Shiromani Akali Dal") nominee which he subsequently won defeating [Jagbir Singh Brar](/wiki/Jagbir_Singh_Brar "Jagbir Singh Brar") of Congress.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

He was suspended by [SAD](/wiki/Shiromani_Akali_Dal "Shiromani Akali Dal") in July 2016.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

He along with [Navjot Singh Sidhu](/wiki/Navjot_Singh_Sidhu "Navjot Singh Sidhu") and Bains brothers formed a new political front - Aawaaz-e-Punjab claiming to fight against those working against Punjab.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Congress

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pargat_Singh&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Congress")]

Singh was one of the 42 INC MLAs who submitted their resignation in protest of a decision of the [Supreme Court of India](/wiki/Supreme_Court_of_India "Supreme Court of India") ruling Punjab's termination of the Sutlej-Yamuna Link (SYL) water canal unconstitutional.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

He has been a Congress MLA since 2017 from the Jalandhar Cantt constituency. He has been nominated again by Jalandhar Cantt for elections 2022. He is going to contest the Punjab assembly elections for the third time in a row.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The Aam Aadmi Party gained a strong 79% majority in the [sixteenth Punjab Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Sixteenth_Punjab_Legislative_Assembly "Sixteenth Punjab Legislative Assembly") by winning 92 out of [117 seats](/wiki/List_of_constituencies_of_the_Punjab_Legislative_Assembly "List of constituencies of the Punjab Legislative Assembly") in the [2022 Punjab Legislative Assembly election](/wiki/2022_Punjab_Legislative_Assembly_election "2022 Punjab Legislative Assembly election"). MP [Bhagwant Mann](/wiki/Bhagwant_Mann "Bhagwant Mann") was sworn in as [Chief Minister](/wiki/List_of_chief_ministers_of_Punjab_(India) "List of chief ministers of Punjab (India)") on 16 March 2022.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 2017, he was nominated as the General Secretary of Hockey Punjab.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
